prompt: Bench stations keep going dark after a bad diagnostics pass. The hub says the device handle is still checked out, so every later pass on that device queues forever — we're power-cycling the hub daily now. Two symptoms, both out of withCapture in capture.ts: first, when the capture engine refuses to start a capture, the device handle never goes back to the hub. Second, when a capture's stop() fails after a failing pass, the log shows 'stop timed out' and the actual probe assertion that failed the pass is just gone — QA burned half a day chasing the wrong error, and in that situation the handle doesn't come back either. Callers already pass an onCleanupError hook to withCapture; disposal problems belong there and must never clobber a pass result or replace the pass error. One of our work callbacks also throws synchronously (bad probe map) and that has to behave exactly like a rejected pass. Fix capture.ts. `node --test test_capture.ts` is the contract; leave the tests alone.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: Build/import of capture.ts exits 0. node --test exits 1 with 4 pass / 5 fail: when startCapture rejects the injected rig records closed=0 (handle leaked), a stop() rejection surfaces instead of the primary pass error and skips handle.close (closed=0 again), and a stop failure after a good pass rejects the whole call instead of returning the result.
